official_functions.role: Construct the official custom query payload Identifier fixture
Construct an official empty login custom query payload fixture
Construct the official Login clientbound custom_query packet fixture
Write the official Login clientbound custom_query body through transactionId VarInt, Identifier, and empty payload body
Write the full Login clientbound framed custom_query packet
Dispatch and decode the full Login clientbound framed custom_query packet
Name the packet id assigned in Login clientbound state
Read decoded Login custom_query fields and confirm the clientbound listener surface

invariants: The official Login clientbound protocol codec writes the packet table id before the custom_query body.
The official Login clientbound protocol codec dispatches the framed packet id to ClientboundCustomQueryPacket.
The official ClientboundCustomQueryPacket body is transactionId as FriendlyByteBuf VarInt, payload id as FriendlyByteBuf Identifier, then payload.write(FriendlyByteBuf).
The official DiscardedQueryPayload fixture writes an empty payload body.
The decoded official ClientboundCustomQueryPacket preserves transactionId and payload id from the fixture.

The framed bytes, packet table id, body bytes, decoded packet class, decoded packet type, decoded fields, Login clientbound packet table, and remaining decode byte count are generated by the official jar-backed answer artifact.

This contract does not prove plugin channel handling, custom query semantics, login acknowledgement behavior, Login-to-Configuration state transition handling, Configuration entry, Play readiness, or client-load completion.
test_topology_note: rust_test_target names the current case adapter only; the manifest owns the runnable test topology.
stop_boundary: Do not use this framed dispatch/decode contract to infer plugin channel handling, custom query semantics, login acknowledgement behavior, Login-to-Configuration state transition handling, Configuration entry, Play readiness, or client-load completion.
